questionHtml: State two features showing that propane and butane are members of the same homologous series.
markschemeHtml:
same general formula / CnH2n+2 ✔

differ by CH2/common structural unit ✔

 

Accept "similar chemical properties".

Accept “gradation/gradual change in physical properties”.

meta.title: State two features showing that propane and butane are members of the same homologous series.
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 3.2—Functional groups: Classification of organic compounds

Structure 3.2.3—A homologous series is a family of compounds in which successive members differ by a common structural unit, typically CH2. Each homologous series can be described by a general formula. Identify the following homologous series: alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, halogenoalkanes, alcohols, aldehydes, ketones, carboxylic acids, ethers, amines, amides and esters.
